briefing.opening: Good morning/afternoon. I will now begin the climate briefing for March 3, 2026.

sections.title: 🇰🇷 Expanding Domestic Efforts for Carbon Neutrality and Climate Change Response
sections.content: Domestic local governments and institutions are actively expanding their efforts to raise awareness of climate change and achieve carbon neutrality. Cheongju's 'Companion Plant Hospital - Climate Change Experience Center' has reopened after renovation, providing a venue for climate change education to citizens [1, 7]. In Geumcheon-gu, a carbon neutrality practice flea market is being held [10, 27, 29], and Gwangmyeong City is fostering 'Youth Climate Soldiers' and 'Youth Climate Academies' as future carbon neutrality leaders [18, 26]. Furthermore, various entities, including Ulsan Buk-gu General Social Welfare Center and Usisan, are collaborating to spread a resident-participatory carbon-neutral culture [23, 25], demonstrating broad participation in climate action. Gwangmyeong City is introducing the concept of carbon neutrality into urban regeneration [16], and the Hanam Citizens' Alliance aims for a carbon-neutral AI city in Gyo-san New Town [31], indicating that carbon neutrality is becoming a key element in urban planning. Companies are also contributing, with Ilji Tech Co., Ltd. receiving the highest rating in the CDP Climate Change Response assessment [3].

sections.title: 🇰🇷 Accelerating Renewable Energy Transition and Policy Discussions
sections.content: The Republic of Korea is accelerating the adoption of renewable energy and related policy discussions. Wonju City is contributing to carbon neutrality by introducing eco-friendly LNG at Camp Long Citizens' Park [9, 17, 30]. Incheon City is also actively promoting the use of new and renewable energy by participating in 'Sunlight Income' [33]. Gangneung City has been selected for the new and renewable energy convergence and complex public offering project for four consecutive years and plans to fully launch a complex support project investing a total of 3 billion KRW in 2026 [34, 35, 37, 40]. The Korea Photovoltaic Industry Association anticipates improvements in the investment environment and industrial advancement [38]. Amidst these efforts, Tongyeong City is re-examining nuclear energy as a key means for carbon neutrality through 'Energy Safety Education' [14]. Policy discussions on the energy mix are active, with the necessity of introducing seasonal/temporal distribution for highly variable renewable energy also being raised [41]. Major companies such as Daewoong, Kwangdong, and JW Pharmaceutical are also accelerating energy independence through the introduction of solar and combined heat and power generation [39].

sections.title: ⚠️ Global Climate Change Threat and Increased Vulnerability
sections.content: The world is facing increased vulnerability and direct impacts from climate change, strongly indicating the need for immediate action. Michelangelo's 'Last Judgment' and the Sistine Chapel masterpieces have necessitated restoration work due to visitors' breath, sweat, and climate change [44, 47]. The Alps are also in crisis [42]. Reports that New York City's bridges are corroding due to climate change [48] demonstrate the vulnerability of essential infrastructure. Predictions that cattle, sheep, and goat grazing lands could halve by 2100 [50] add to concerns about food security. According to one study, 77% of U.S. national parks are highly vulnerable to climate change [56], and new maps have precisely identified the parks most severely affected [60]. The World Health Organization (WHO) warned that 80% of Mexico's population is at health risk due to extreme events caused by climate change [66], emphasizing that climate change is having detrimental effects not only on ecosystem disruption but also on human health [61].

sections.title: 🌍 International Community Seeks Climate Change Response Strategies and Urges Action
sections.content: The international community is exploring various strategies and urging action to address climate change, though there are also observations that related reporting is decreasing [49]. Scientists are calling for an international assessment of climate change risks and obligations [58], and at the International Court of Justice (ICJ), discussions are underway regarding an advisory opinion on the causal link of climate change [51]. Interestingly, research has shown that hairdressers can play a significant role in addressing climate change through conversations with their clients [43, 55]. In San Luis Obispo, volunteers are tackling climate change by tending to urban forests [52]. Scotland is reviewing its draft climate change plan for 2026 to 2040 [62, 64], and Greater Manchester is showing progress towards achieving its 2038 carbon neutrality target [69]. Furthermore, Norway's sovereign wealth fund has made its first investment in U.S. renewable energy assets [72], and a London borough is proposing legislation to expand solar power generation for low-income households [65], indicating ongoing concrete action plans and investments. However, a UN expert criticized the global economy for catering to the 'frivolous and destructive demands' of the ultra-rich, leading to inequality and ecological collapse [70], emphasizing the need for fundamental systemic change.

briefing.closing: Today's briefing was an opportunity to reaffirm the severity of the global threat, alongside various domestic and international efforts to address climate change. Everyone's attention and participation are crucial for a sustainable future. This concludes today's climate briefing.

articles.title: Global economy must stop pandering to ‘frivolous desires of ultra-rich’, says UN expert
articles.description: Olivier De Schutter says new economic agenda needed to tackle crises of rising inequality and ecological collapseThe global economy must be reordered to ensure it serves ordinary people around the world rather than the “frivolous and destructive demands of the ultra-rich”, according to a leading UN figure.Olivier De Schutter, the UN special rapporteur on extreme poverty and human rights, says politicians must stop prioritising “socially and ecologically destructive growth” that only increases th

summary.phenomenon: According to UN expert Olivier De Schutter, the current global economy is contributing to deepening inequality and ecological collapse, catering to the frivolous and destructive demands of the ultra-rich.
summary.cause: Politicians are prioritizing 'socially and ecologically destructive growth,' which is driven by the demands of the ultra-rich, thereby exacerbating inequality and destroying ecosystems.
summary.outlook: To address these crises, a new economic agenda for ordinary people is required, which could bring about positive change through the reordering of the global economy.

articles.title: Historic harvests and sky-high prices – so why can’t Colombia’s coffee-growers hire pickers?
articles.description: Though coffee is one of the world’s most important commodities, little of the profit trickles down to the farmers, while workers are abandoning the countryside in search of more lucrative jobs in the cityMary Luz Pérez Arrubla and her brother, Rodrigo, are fourth-generation farmers cultivating coffee on steep Andean slopes near the town of Líbano, in the rich agricultural region of Tolima. Along with the rest of Colombia, the family has enjoyed a historic harvest amid surging global coffee price

summary.phenomenon: Across Colombia, including farmers cultivating coffee on Andean slopes near Líbano in the Tolima region, a historic harvest has been experienced amidst surging global coffee prices.
summary.cause: The cause is attributed to the limited share of profits from coffee, a globally significant commodity, reaching farmers, coupled with laborers abandoning rural areas in pursuit of more lucrative employment opportunities in urban centers.
